note: Sprint 5.19: Trinity Useful Compute Operator Loop v0.1. New scripts/trinity/useful_compute_operator_loop.py orchestrates the full Useful Compute pipeline end-to-end (task_builder -> worker x N -> replay_validator -> governance_gate -> reward_budget_policy -> payment_proposal -> payment_draft) through importlib calls to its seven sibling scripts. No subprocess, no sost-cli reference, no wallet/private-key tokens, no real-signing path. The loop is deterministic given --pinned-time and resumable via --resume operator_run.json with per-artifact sha256 verification (tampered artefact aborts the resume with rc=2). New schema useful-compute-operator-run/v0.1 locks allow_wallet_access=const false, allow_broadcast=const false and human_review_required=const true. Operator confirmation token (exact match): I_UNDERSTAND_THIS_IS_ONLY_A_DRY_RUN_LOOP. Pre-argparse rejection of --broadcast, --send, --payout-now, --auto-pay, --sign-now, --export-private-key, --wallet, --from-label, --from-address, --allow-wallet-access, --allow-broadcast with rc=2. Tests: 12 static safety, 8 schema, 21 functional (happy path + resume + tamper detection + 11 forbidden-flag matrix + determinism + schema validation). Web (trinity-useful-compute.html) adds a small Operator Loop v0.1 card; no fetch / no wallet inputs / no real-sign references. No consensus, RPC, wallet, signer or wire-format change.
